title: Drafts List
description: Shelved work is invisible to the active change list, so users need a dedicated view to see what has been parked. `specd drafts list` lists all changes currently in `drafts/`, sorted by `createdAt` ascending.
context: Shelved work is invisible to the active change list, so users need a dedicated view to see what has been parked. `specd drafts list` lists all changes currently in `drafts/`, sorted by `createdAt` ascending.

rules:
```
specd drafts list [--format text|json|toon]
```

Alias:

```
specd draft list [--format text|json|toon]
```

No positional arguments. The `--format` flag controls output encoding; defaults to `text`.

The command MUST invoke `ListDrafts.execute()` and treat each entry as a `DraftedChangeView`.

The command MUST read `history` from each view to extract the latest `drafted` event for `draftedAt`, `draftedBy`, and `reason`.

The command MUST NOT assume entries are mutable `Change` instances.

The command prints a human-readable table to stdout. Changes are sorted by `createdAt` ascending (oldest first).

The output has an inverse-video column header row `NAME  STATE  DATE  BY  REASON` above the data rows. Column widths are fixed at render time, computed from the widest value across all rows for each column (global, not per-group). The REASON column uses wrap overflow when its content exceeds the column width.

When `--format json` is passed, the command writes a JSON array to stdout:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "...",
    "state": "...",
    "draftedAt": "...",
    "draftedBy": { "name": "...", "email": "..." },
    "reason": "..."
  }
]
```

One object per drafted change, sorted by `createdAt` ascending. `reason` is omitted when not provided. `draftedBy` is omitted when not recorded.
When `--format toon` is passed, the command writes the same data model as JSON encoded in Token-Oriented Object Notation (toon) to stdout.
If there are no changes in `drafts/`, the command prints `no drafts` to stdout in text mode, or `[]` in JSON/toon mode, and exits with code 0.
- If an I/O error occurs while reading `drafts/`, the command exits with code 3 and prints an `error:` message to stderr.

constraints: Errors always go to stderr as plain text regardless of --format

scenarios.name: No positional arguments
then: the command proceeds without positional arguments
AND output format defaults to text

scenarios.name: Invokes ListDrafts
then: ListDrafts.execute is called

scenarios.name: Does not invoke GetStatus per row
then: GetStatus is not invoked for each listed name

scenarios.name: JSON output serializes view fields
given: ListDrafts returns views with name and isDrafted === true
then: stdout is a JSON array whose objects include name
AND entries do not include mutable Change internals

scenarios.name: TOON format output
when: specd drafts list --format toon is run
then: output is encoded in Token-Oriented Object Notation (toon)

scenarios.name: Drafts listed with correct fields
given: drafts/ contains two changes: old-experiment (state drafting, drafted 2024-01-05, actor alice, reason "parked for later") and shelved-work (state designing, drafted 2024-01-03, actor bob, no reason)
then: stdout contains one row for each change showing name, bracketed state, date, and actor
AND the row for old-experiment includes "parked for later"
AND the row for shelved-work does not include a quoted reason
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: Rows sorted by createdAt ascending
given: drafts/ contains change b-change created before a-change (alphabetically later but older)
then: b-change appears before a-change in the output

scenarios.name: Singular alias invocation
then: it behaves as specd drafts list
AND the process exits with the same code as the canonical invocation

scenarios.name: JSON format output
given: drafts/ contains one change named old-experiment in state drafting, drafted at 2024-01-05T10:00:00.000Z, actor alice, reason "parked for later"
when: specd drafts list --format json is run
then: stdout is a JSON array with one object containing name, state, draftedAt, draftedBy, and reason fields
AND the object does not contain specIds or schema fields
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: JSON format output — no reason or actor
given: drafts/ contains one change named shelved-work with no reason and no draftedBy recorded
when: specd drafts list --format json is run
then: the JSON object for shelved-work does not contain reason or draftedBy fields
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: No drafted changes — text
then: stdout contains no drafts
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: No drafted changes — JSON
when: specd drafts list --format json is run
then: stdout is []
AND the process exits with code 0

scenarios.name: I/O error reading drafts directory
given: the drafts/ directory cannot be read due to a permissions error
then: the command exits with code 3
AND stderr contains an error: message
